assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md contains two clearly separated root cause sections: 'Root Cause 1: Convention reference formatter (shared/convention-utils.md)' describing the toLowerCase() + kebab-case transform issue, and 'Root Cause 2: Task creation issue type (plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)' describing the Feature issue type ID (10142) being used instead of Task. The independence assessment section confirms these are independent root causes in different modules.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md exists and the rationale section states: 'These two problems are independent because: They are located in different modules: shared/convention-utils.md (shared utility layer) vs. plan-feature/SKILL.md (skill-specific task creation logic). They affect different code paths: convention reference formatting pipeline vs. Jira issue creation pipeline.' investigation.md also states: 'Because the investigation identified two independent root causes in different modules... the Decomposition Guard (Step 6) is triggered.'

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents both issues: '1. Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (in shared/convention-utils.md)' and '2. Task creation uses wrong issue type ID (in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)'. Each issue includes the affected file/module in parentheses.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents: 'Options: 1. Proceed — create a single Task covering all fixes 2. Split — I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually Choose (1/2):'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: No task-description.md file exists in eval-3/outputs/ — only investigation.md and decomposition-guard.md were produced. The investigation.md explicitly states: 'The skill must NOT silently create a single Task. Instead, it must present the user with a choice.' The decomposition guard prompt ends with 'Choose (1/2):' indicating it waits for user input before proceeding.
